courses.title: AI Engineering
courses.description: Master the art of building AI-powered applications

lessons.title: What is an AI Engineer?
lessons.description: Understanding the role of an AI Engineer in modern tech teams

content.transcript: AI Engineers are responsible for building, deploying, and maintaining AI systems...

lessons.title: Roles & Responsibilities
lessons.description: Deep dive into what AI Engineers do day-to-day

sections.title: Core Responsibilities
sections.body: AI Engineers design and implement machine learning models, deploy AI systems at scale, and collaborate with data scientists and software engineers.

sections.title: Team Collaboration
sections.body: Work closely with product managers, data scientists, and DevOps teams to deliver AI-powered features.

sections.title: Technical Skills Required
sections.body: Proficiency in Python, TensorFlow/PyTorch, cloud platforms, and MLOps practices.

lessons.title: Impact on Product Development
lessons.description: How AI is transforming product development

content.summary: AI is revolutionizing how products are built, tested, and delivered to users.
content.body: AI integration in product development leads to smarter features, automated workflows, and personalized user experiences. Companies are leveraging AI to reduce costs, improve efficiency, and create competitive advantages.

lessons.title: AI vs ML Engineering
lessons.description: Understanding the difference between AI and ML Engineering

content.topic: AI Engineering vs Machine Learning Engineering: What's the difference?

aiEngineering.definition: Building applications that use AI capabilities

mlEngineering.definition: Creating and optimizing machine learning models

content.discussion: Both roles are crucial for building AI products. While ML Engineers focus on model development, AI Engineers ensure these models work effectively in production.

lessons.title: Common AI Terminology
lessons.description: Essential terms every AI Engineer should know

terms.definition: A subset of AI where systems learn from data without explicit programming
terms.example: Spam detection that learns from user reports

terms.definition: Computing systems inspired by biological neural networks
terms.example: Deep learning models for image recognition

terms.definition: The process of teaching a model from data
terms.example: Feeding labeled images to recognize cats

terms.definition: Using a trained model to make predictions
terms.example: Running a model on new images to detect objects

terms.definition: Settings that control the learning process
terms.example: Learning rate, batch size, number of layers

terms.definition: When a model learns training data too well
terms.example: High accuracy on training but poor on test data

terms.definition: Using knowledge from one task for another
terms.example: Using ImageNet pre-trained models for new tasks

modules.title: Python Fundamentals for AI
modules.description: Essential Python skills for AI Engineering

modules.title: Natural Language Processing
modules.description: Working with text and language models
